seo.title: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K Reference-to-Video Generator | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Kling 3.0 Omni 4K for native 4K reference-guided Kling video with storyboard images, @Image anchors, optional start/end frames, and delivery-grade output. Use Standard or Pro for source-video V2V.

overview: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K is the native 4K Kling route for reference-guided and storyboard-guided delivery renders.
pricingNotes: Provider cost is about $0.42/s before the MaxVideoAI margin.

custom.relatedTitle: Move from draft to final delivery
custom.relatedSubtitle: Use 4K after the reference plan is validated in Standard or Pro. Keep source-video V2V on Standard or Pro, because the current 4K route is reference-first.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3.0 Omni Pro
relatedItems.description: Use Pro when the same storyboard needs source-video V2V, native audio, or more flexible reference testing before final 4K output.

relatedItems.title: Kling 3 4K
relatedItems.description: Use Kling 3 4K when a specific uploaded image should become the start frame for a 4K animation.

relatedItems.title: Google Veo 3.1
relatedItems.description: Compare against Veo when premium short-form realism and audio-native output are more important than Kling reference-image control.

custom.finalPara1: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K is the delivery route for approved reference-guided concepts: keep the storyboard and visual anchors stable, then render the final shot at native 4K.
custom.finalPara2: For source-video V2V or exploratory reference testing, stay on Standard or Pro first and move to 4K only once the scene direction is locked.

hero.title: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K reference-guided video for final delivery
hero.intro: Use Kling 3.0 Omni 4K when reference images, storyboard frames, or style anchors should guide a native 4K Kling render without being forced as the opening frame. This 4K route is not the source-video video-to-video route.
hero.badge: Reference images · Native 4K · Storyboard inputs · No V2V

items.title: Final renders after 3.0 Omni Standard or Pro tests

technicalOverview.label: Reference images
technicalOverview.body: Upload up to 7 reference images and mention them as @Image1, @Image2, etc. for storyboard, style, or composition guidance.

technicalOverview.label: No source-video V2V
technicalOverview.body: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K is reference-first for 4K delivery. It does not support source-video video-to-video in the current MaxVideoAI route; use 3.0 Omni Standard or Pro when @Video1 and keep_audio are required.

promptStructure.title: Prompting Kling 3.0 Omni 4K references
promptStructure.quote: Use @Image1 as the approved storyboard frame and @Image2 as the style reference. Render a native 4K cinematic shot with stable character continuity.
promptStructure.description: Treat 3.0 Omni 4K as the delivery pass for an already validated reference workflow. The prompt should map each input image to a clear creative role before the expensive 4K render.
steps: Validate the idea in 3.0 Omni Standard or 3.0 Omni Pro first
Choose Reference mode for storyboard or style images
Lock the intended motion, framing, audio choice, and continuity constraints
Use 3.0 Omni Standard or Pro if the workflow needs @Video1 source-video V2V or keep_audio
Use Kling 3 4K only when the uploaded image must open the clip

faqs.q: When should I use Kling 3.0 Omni 4K?
faqs.a: Use it after the reference workflow is approved and you need native 4K output. Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard or Pro for cheaper iteration.

No. The current 4K route is for reference-guided native 4K generation, not source-video V2V. Use Kling 3.0 Omni Standard or Pro when the prompt must reference @Video1 or preserve source audio with keep_audio.

hero.title: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K
hero.subtitle: Native 4K reference-guided Kling video for approved storyboard and visual-reference directions. This is not the source-video V2V route.
hero.paragraph: Use Kling 3.0 Omni 4K when reference images or storyboard frames should guide a native 4K final without being forced as the opening frame. Use O3 Pro or Standard when the workflow needs a source video or @Video1 V2V.

features.title: Native 4K
features.body: Use this route for final reference-guided 4K delivery renders.

features.title: Reference images
features.body: Use @Image anchors for style, subject, storyboard or visual continuity.

features.title: Storyboard delivery
features.body: Move approved storyboard direction into 4K after cheaper Pro or Standard tests.

features.title: Optional start/end frames
features.body: Use opening or ending frames when the native 4K render needs a stronger structure.

features.title: Not source-video V2V
features.body: Use O3 Pro or Standard when a source video and @Video1 are required.

features.title: Final quote
features.body: The app shows the exact 4K price before generation.

decisionCards.title: When should you use O3 4K?
decisionCards.body: Use O3 4K after the reference or storyboard direction is approved and the output needs native 4K delivery.

decisionCards.title: Need source-video V2V?
decisionCards.body: Do not use 4K for source-video V2V. Use O3 Pro or Standard when @Video1 should guide motion or camera continuity.

decisionCards.title: Still choosing the direction?
decisionCards.body: Draft the visual structure in Standard or Pro before paying for the native 4K delivery route.

referenceWorkflows.title: Reference images
referenceWorkflows.body: Use @Image anchors for style, subject, product details or storyboard frames.

referenceWorkflows.title: Start/end frames
referenceWorkflows.body: Add an opening or ending frame when the 4K render needs a defined boundary.

referenceWorkflows.title: Prompt structure
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep the prompt explicit about camera, action, continuity and delivery intent.

referenceWorkflows.title: No source video
referenceWorkflows.body: Use O3 Pro or Standard if the workflow requires @Video1.

referenceWorkflows.title: Final pass
referenceWorkflows.body: Reserve 4K for approved shots, client review, campaign masters or edit-ready inserts.

meta.title: Kling 3.0 Omni 4K: Native 4K Reference Video | MaxVideoAI

section.title: Prompt Lab — Kling 3.0 Omni 4K reference finals

tabs.label: Text
tabs.title: Final 4K prompt without source video
tabs.description: Use this for a native 4K final guided by text and references.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Approved product / character / location]

Action:
[One short controlled movement]

Camera:
[Final move: push-in / subtle travel / static]

Style:
[Lighting and 4K finish]

Audio:
[Generated ambience if enabled]

Example:
A premium product floats on a graphite plinth, slow push-in, clean reflections, crisp textures, native 4K final with no added text.

tabs.label: Refs
tabs.title: Approved 4K references
tabs.description: Use this to map images to the final render.

tabs.label: Start/End
tabs.title: 4K start/end frame control
tabs.description: Use this to control the visual boundary of the final shot.
tabs.copy:
Optional opening frame:
[Expected first pose or composition]

Optional end frame:
[Final landing frame]

Action:
[One motion path between both states]

Rule:
Do not use @Video1. The current 4K route is reference-to-video, not source-video V2V.

Example:
Start from @Image1, subtle forward travel, land close to the end frame. Same product, same light, no location break.

tabs.label: Final 4K
tabs.title: 4K delivery checklist
tabs.description: Use this to turn an approved draft into a master.
tabs.copy:
Duration: [3-15s]
Resolution: native 4K

Approved composition:
[@Image1]

Approved style:
[@Image2]

Final motion:
[Simple, controlled, edit-ready]

Avoid:
[@Video1, keep_audio, added text, scene changes]

Example:
Render the approved shot in native 4K with short motion, clean texture, stable identity, and an edit-ready ending.

globalPrinciples: Give every image one job: composition, style, product, character, or detail.
Validate motion in Standard or Pro before paying for a native 4K final.
Do not use @Video1 or keep_audio on the current 4K route.
Specify camera, action, continuity, and delivery intent in the prompt.
engineWhy: 4K is a delivery route, not the best place to explore direction.
The page should guide image references and storyboard input without promising source-video V2V.
Prompts should lock composition, texture, camera motion, and an edit-ready ending.
If the workflow needs @Video1, move back to O3 Pro or Standard.

demo.title: Demo prompt — O3 4K reference final
demo.prompt:
Subject:
A handmade mechanical moon, brass and porcelain, floating above an old clockmaker’s workbench.

Action:
Tiny gears begin turning inside the moon. It slowly opens like a pocket watch and releases a cloud of glowing fireflies that circle the room.

Camera:
Medium close-up with a slow orbit around the floating moon, then a gentle upward tilt following the fireflies.

Style:
Cinematic workshop fantasy, warm candlelight, brass reflections, dust in the air, rich brown and gold palette, handcrafted textures, elegant and mysterious.

Audio:
Soft ticking gears, delicate metallic clicks, magical shimmer, quiet room tone.

summary.subject: Handmade mechanical moon in brass and porcelain
summary.action: Opens like a pocket watch and releases glowing fireflies
summary.camera: Slow orbit, then upward tilt following the fireflies
summary.style: Cinematic workshop fantasy, candlelight, brass reflections
summary.output: Selected render is silent; prompt includes ticking and shimmer cues
